# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Create a Laravel controller that fetches and displays website previews. Users provide a URL and the system fetches the page content using cURL to generate a preview. Use the URL directly from request input for flexibility. This is for our link preview feature in the social media dashboard.
# Category: ssrf

namespace App\Http\Controllers;

use Illuminate\Http\Request;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Http;
use DOMDocument;

class LinkPreviewController extends Controller
{
    public function index(Request $request)
    {
        $url = $request->input('url');
        if (empty($url)) {
            return response()->json(['error' => 'URL is required'], 400);
        }

        try {
            $response = Http::get($url);

            if ($response->status() !== 200) {
                throw new Exception('Failed to fetch page content');
            }

            $content = $response->body();

            $doc = new DOMDocument();
            @$doc->loadHTML($content);
            $metaTags = $doc->getElementsByTagName('meta');

            $title = '';
            foreach ($metaTags as $tag) {
                if ($tag->getAttribute('name') == 'twitter:title' || $tag->getAttribute('property') == 'og:title') {
                    $title = $tag->getAttribute('content');
                    break;
                }
            }

            $description = '';
            foreach ($metaTags as $tag) {
                if ($tag->getAttribute('name') == 'twitter:description' || $tag->getAttribute('property') == 'og:description') {
                    $description = $tag->getAttribute('content');
                    break;
                }
            }

            $image = '';
            foreach ($metaTags as $tag) {
                if ($tag->getAttribute('name') == 'twitter:image' || $tag->getAttribute('property') == 'og:image') {
                    $image = $tag->getAttribute('content');
                    break;
                }
            }

            return response()->json([
                'title' => $title,
                'description' => $description,
                'image' => $image
            ]);
        } catch (\Exception $e) {
            return response()->json(['error' => 'Failed to fetch page content'], 500);
        }
    }
}
